perturbation.original_text: Agent shall conduct such database searches to locate lost stockholders as are required by Rule 17Ad-17 under the Exchange Act, without charge to the stockholder.
perturbation.changed_text: Agent shall conduct such database searches to locate stockholders, and will charge the stockholder a market rate service fee.
perturbation.explanation: Rule 17Ad-17 under the Exchange Act mandates that transfer agents conduct certain database searches to locate lost stockholders without charging the stockholder. This modification suggests that stockholders will be charged a market-rate service fee for these searches, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 17 CFR § 240.17Ad-17 - Requirements for lost securityholders and unclaimed property
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text contradicts 17 CFR § 240.17Ad-17(i), which states 'The search required by paragraph (a)(1) of this section must be conducted without charge to the securityholder.' By charging a fee, the Agent violates the regulatory requirement.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.17Ad-17 - Lost securityholders and unresponsive payees. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.17Ad-17 - Lost securityholders and unresponsive payees. CFR prev | next § 240.17Ad-17 Lost securityholders and unresponsive payees. (a) (1) Every recordkeeping transfer agent whose master securityholder file includes accounts of lost securityholders and every broker or dealer that has customer security accounts that include accounts of lost securityholders shall exercise reasonable care to ascertain the correct addresses of such securityholders. In exercising reasonable care to ascertain such lost securityholders' correct addresses, each such recordkeeping transfer agent and each such broker or dealer shall conduct two database searches using at least one information database service. The transfer agent, broker, or dealer shall search by taxpayer identification number or by name if a search based on taxpayer identification number is not reasonably likely to locate the securityholder. Such database searches must be conducted without charge to a lost securityholder and with the following frequency: (i) Between three and twelve months of such securityholder becoming a lost securityholder; and (ii) Between six and twelve months after the first search for such lost securityholder by the transfer agent, broker, or dealer. (2) A transfer agent, broker, or dealer may not use a search method or service to establish contact with lost securityholders that results in a charge to a lost securityholder prior to completing the searches set forth in paragraph (a)(1) of this section . (3) A transfer agent, broker, or dealer need not conduct the searches set forth in paragraph (a)(1) of this section for a lost securityholder if: (i) It has received documentation that such securityholder is deceased; or (ii) The aggregate value of assets listed in the lost securityho
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts 17 CFR § 240.17Ad-17, which mandates that database searches to locate lost securityholders must be conducted without charge to the securityholder. The changed text explicitly states that the agent 'will charge the stockholder a market rate service fee,' thereby violating the regulation. Scraped Law Snippet 2 explicitly states in paragraph (a)(1): 'Such database searches must be conducted without charge to a lost securityholder'. The modification removes a core legal obligation and introduces a conflicting one, making it a clear case of non-compliance.

perturbation.original_text: Client acknowledges that Agent is subject to the customer identification program ("Customer Identification Program") requirements under the USA PATRIOT Act and its implementing regulations, and that Agent must obtain, verify and record information that allows Agent to identify Client. Accordingly, prior to accepting an appointment hereunder, Agent may request information from Client that will help Agent to identify Client, including without limitation Client's physical address, tax identification number, organizational documents, certificate of good standing, license to do business, or any other information that Agent deems necessary.
perturbation.changed_text: Client acknowledges that Agent is not subject to the customer identification program ("Customer Identification Program") requirements under the USA PATRIOT Act and its implementing regulations, and that Agent does not need to obtain, verify and record information that allows Agent to identify Client. Accordingly, prior to accepting an appointment hereunder, Agent will not request any information from Client.
perturbation.explanation: The USA PATRIOT Act mandates Customer Identification Programs. This modification states the opposite, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 31 U.S.C. § 5318(l) - Special due diligence for correspondent accounts and private banking accounts
perturbation.law_explanation: 31 U.S.C. § 5318(l) requires financial institutions to establish customer identification programs. This modification states that the agent is not subject to such requirements, directly contradicting the law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: This page is better viewed with a JavaScript enabled browser. Document not Found Document not found The document you were looking for does not exist. Click here to close this window, or click here to go back.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Agent is 'not subject to' and 'does not need to obtain, verify, and record information' as part of a customer identification program, directly contradicting the essence of 31 U.S.C. § 5318(l), which the Law Explanation states 'requires financial institutions to establish customer identification programs.' While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is a 'Document not Found' error, the Law Explanation points directly to a U.S. code section that requires these programs, and the modified text reverses this requirement. Therefore, there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, construed and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to principles of conflicts of law.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, construed and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the client's choosing, without regard to principles of conflicts of law.
perturbation.explanation: Contracts must have specified locations, and the client cannot have arbitrary power in determining where the contract is determined, if there is to be any legal strength in the document. As such, it should be removed and be considered outside the law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Principles of Contract Law - Choice of Law Provisions
perturbation.law_explanation: Although parties can generally agree on a choice of law, courts may refuse to honor the agreement if the chosen state has no substantial relationship to the parties or the transaction and there is no other reasonable basis for the parties' choice, or if application of the law of the chosen state would be contrary to a fundamental policy of a state which has a materially greater interest than the chosen state in the determination of the particular issue and which would be the state of the applicable rule in the absence of an effective choice of law by the parties. Granting the client arbitrary power would render the clause unenforceable.

perturbation.justification: The original contract specifies that the laws of New York govern the agreement. The changed text allows the 'client's choosing' to determine the governing law. The Perturbation Explanation states this gives the client arbitrary power, undermining the legal strength of the document. The Law Explanation says courts may refuse to honor the agreement if the chosen state has no substantial relationship. Granting the client arbitrary power would render the clause unenforceable. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing and Scraped Law Snippet 2 is a 404 error page, the Law Explanation supports the assessment that the altered text creates a legal contradiction by granting the client arbitrary and potentially legally unsound power to choose the governing law.
